stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 3 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 20 observations with mean 72.8 % (within-factory SD = 7.4). factory B has 10 observations with mean 63.7 % (within-factory SD = 7.7). factory C has 20 observations with mean 87.7 % (within-factory SD = 7.9). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.6 and 21.6 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory C: 87.5 % (shrunk from raw 87.7). Range [87.3, 87.6] as between-factory SD varies from 8.6 to 21.6. Pooled mean: 76.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 76.9 % (weighted average of 3 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=14.4: B = 0.985.
Interval [87.3, 87.6] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (87.7) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (76.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
